Scenario.title: J-Magic Campaign: Targeting Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A basic-level threat group leverages phishing and public-facing application exploitation to disrupt critical infrastructure, culminating in denial-of-service attacks against traffic control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Network Traffic Content

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-20035 (Suggested): SonicWall SMA100 appliances contain an OS command injection vulnerability in the management interface that allows a remote authenticated attacker to inject arbitrary commands as a 'nobody' user, which could potentially lead to code execution.
stage.description: The attacker deploys phishing emails containing malicious links to compromise employee credentials, gaining initial access to internet-facing systems. This aligns with CAPEC-164, leveraging human error to bypass authentication mechanisms.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-35730 (Suggested): Roundcube Webmail contains a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability that allows an attacker to send a plain text e-mail message with Javascript in a link reference element that is mishandled by linkref_addinindex in rcube_string_replacer.php.
stage.description: Using the compromised credentials, the attacker exploits an unpatched Roundcube Webmail instance to inject malicious scripts into application logs. This enables execution of arbitrary code, establishing persistence on the targeted infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker floods network traffic with malformed packets, overwhelming infrastructure and causing disruptions to traffic signal controls. This aligns with CAPEC-583, targeting physical network components to disable critical operational functions.

Scenario.summary: A basic threat group exploits unpatched systems and human error to execute a multi-stage attack, ultimately disrupting transportation infrastructure through denial-of-service techniques.
